news.title: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady at April 28-29 FOMC Meeting
news.desc: The Federal Reserve is widely expected to leave interest rates unchanged at its April 29 policy meeting, with market pricing showing near-zero probability of a rate cut. The meeting concludes today (April 29), with the statement and dot plot release closely watched for signals on the balance sheet trajectory and reserve management purchases.

news.title: Fed H.4.1 Report: Reserve Balances Update (April 23, 2026)
news.desc: The Federal Reserve's latest Factors Affecting Reserve Balances (H.4.1) report for the week ending April 23 provides updated data on bank reserves, the Treasury General Account (TGA), and reverse repo facility usage — key inputs for tracking USD net liquidity trends.

news.title: CIBC: Market Overestimating Fed QT Impact, Pace Will Be 'Slower and More Gradual'
news.desc: CIBC analysis argues the market has overestimated the tightening impact of the Fed's balance sheet runoff, expecting the pace to be slower and more gradual than priced in. The bank sees the SOFR-IOER forward spread as slightly too wide, suggesting the Fed is unlikely to adjust reserve or ON RRP rates in the near term.
